title: Transverse curvature of log |ξ| on the critical line: a reproducible numerical study
author: Lucero Sánchez, Rogelio
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
We study the transverse curvature 
 is the completed Riemann zeta function. The quantity C(t) measures the local curvature of 
 log |ξ(s)| in the direction perpendicular to the critical line. We present a reproducible highprecision numerical protocol based on central finite differences, stability tests in the step size, and 
 optional validation by logarithmic derivatives. A two-level sampling strategy is used: logarithmic 
 sampling on a wide interval and refined sampling near the first non-trivial zero ordinates. The 
 computation is intended as a quantitative numerical probe of the geometry of ξ, not as a proof 
 of the Riemann Hypothesis. We also record the elementary implication that, if the Riemann 
 Hypothesis holds, then C(t) > 0 for every t which is not a zero ordinate, while C(t) → +∞ as t 
 approaches such an ordinate.
